stories.title: Iran hits Gulf refineries as Trump warns U.S. will attack Iranian bridges, power plants
stories.description: Iranian officials said one of the longest bridges linking Tehran to the city of Karaj was destroyed overnight, while Iranian missiles and drones hit Israel, Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait's largest oil refinery, setting some units on fire.

stories.title: Evacuation of U.S. troops from Mideast base sends community groups scrambling to help
stories.description: Troops and their families have been pushed back to the United States after their bases in the Middle East were threatened by Iranian counterattacks. Community groups are scrambling to react.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz closure deflates global helium supply
stories.description: The blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is affecting not only oil, but the global helium supply.

stories.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
stories.description: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

stories.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
stories.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

stories.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
stories.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes destroy Iranian highway bridge
stories.description: It is the tallest bridge in the Middle East, according to Iranian media. The B1 bridge only 35km of Tehran was destroyed in two US-Israeli strikes on April 2. Trump threatened energy plants could be next if Iran didn't agree to a deal to end the conflict.

stories.title: UN Security Council to vote on authorizing force to protect Hormuz
stories.description: The UN Security Council will vote Friday on a draft resolution brought by Bahrain to authorize the use of "defensive" force to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks. Iran has placed a stranglehold on the key shipping lane -- threatening fuel supplies and r...

stories.title: ‘Disturbing trend’ of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets in Iran
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran early Friday, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says there’s been a “disturbing trend” in recent days of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets across Iran. A strike on Iran’s tallest bridge last night killed eight people and wounded 90 others, said Sayah. The...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Istaeli army says will strike two bridges in east Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli army on Friday said it would strike two bridges in east Lebanon. In Iran, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned against any "provocative action" ahead of an expected UN Security Council vote on the use of force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Follow our liveblog for ...

stories.title: Over 100 US legal experts condemn strikes on Iran as possible ‘war crimes’
stories.description: United States-based scholars sign open letter raising concerns about conduct, rhetoric during US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran condemns US-Israeli ‘moral collapse’ after attacks on civilian sites
stories.description: Strikes damage or destroy Iran’s tallest bridge and a century-old medical research centre fighting diseases.

stories.title: Iranian women footballers reveal ‘enormous pressure’ of the Asian Cup saga
stories.description: Two Iranian players who sought asylum in Australia before changing their minds tell their story to Al Jazeera.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 35 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: The US and Israel have hit a century-old medical research centre and a bridge near Tehran.

stories.title: How China’s ‘teapot’ refineries are cushioning it from Iran war oil crisis
stories.description: Small independent refineries are helping China continue importing oil from Iran.

stories.title: Iran war live: Water plant hit in Kuwait as Tehran attacks the Gulf, Israel
stories.description: Water plant hit in Kuwait as Iran warns to expand attacks if Trump follows through on threats to escalate strikes.

stories.title: How war on Iran turned Pakistan’s LNG surplus into a looming shortage
stories.description: Pakistan braces for gas shortage crisis as LNG supplies collapse amid escalating regional conflict.

stories.title: Iran vows retaliation after deadly US strike on bridge in Karaj
stories.description: Iranian officials say the country’s largest bridge will be “built back stronger,” but America’s standing won't recover.

stories.title: Pentagon shake-up as Hegseth ousts army chief amid Iran conflict
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has abruptly forced Army Chief of Staff General Randy George into immediate retirement amid the ongoing war with Iran, multiple US media outlets reported on Friday. The outlets described the move as one of the most consequential Penta...

stories.title: US blows up key Iranian bridge, steel plants
stories.description: • Trump says more will follow after earlier threatening to bomb Iran back to ‘Stone Ages’, claims war nearly over• Tehran vows ‘crushing’ attacks on US after Trump threats• Iran launches missiles towards Israel; fire near Mashhad airport after projectile hits fuel tank• Dozens of...

stories.title: Gulf states seek UN mandate for force to protect Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on Thursday called for the UN Security Council to authorise the use of force to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks. Iran has placed a stranglehold on the key shipping lane — threatening fuel supplies and b...

stories.title: Macron opposes operation to ‘liberate’ Strait, calls it ‘unrealistic’
stories.description: SEOUL: French President Emmanuel Macron said on Thursday that a military operation to liberate the Strait of Hormuz is “unrealistic”, while lamenting Donald Trump’s differing daily statements on the Iran war and Nato. “There are those who advocate for the liberation of the Strait...

stories.title: Luxury stores take hit in Dubai as Iran war drags on
stories.description: DUBAI: Rows of luxury boutiques line an upmarket Dubai mall, yet a month into the Middle East war their sales staff sit idly, phones in hand, watching for the rare customer. Salespeople in immaculate suits say they have been told not to speak to reporters, but one briefly describ...

stories.title: Lebanese PM decries Israel’s ‘far-reaching’ occupation goals
stories.description: • Salam calls Lebanon ‘a victim of war’; condemns Tel Aviv’s ‘dangerous talk’ of buffer zones• 18 European nations demand Israel halt its military campaign, respect Lebanon’s sovereignty BEIRUT: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam declared on Thursday there was no end in sight to...

stories.title: Middle East conflict spiralling into wider regional war, warns UN chief
stories.description: WASHINGTON: UN Secretary General Antó­nio Guterres warned on Thursday that the Middle East crisis was spiralling into a wider regional war, urging the United States and Israel to halt hostilities and calling on Iran to stop attacks on its neighbours. Addressing reporters at UN He...

stories.title: UN experts call for investigation into killing of 3 Lebanese journalists in Israeli strike
stories.description: UN experts have called for an international investigation into the killing of three Lebanese journalists in an Israeli strike, saying Israel had not provided “credible evidence” of their supposed links to armed groups. The three journalists, including Al Manar correspondent Ali S...

stories.title: UK to host 35-state moot on reopening of Hormuz Strait
stories.description: LONDON: Britain will this week hold a meeting of about 35 countries to discuss how to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz which has been crippled by the Middle East war, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Wednesday. British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will host the di...

stories.title: Graveyard raid in India uncovers hidden cooking gas canisters amid shortage
stories.description: Indian police this week seized 414 cooking‑gas canisters hidden in a graveyard in Hyderabad city and arrested those involved in trying to ​sell them on the black market amid shortages due to the ‌US-Israeli war on Iran, a government official said on Thursday. Authorities have ste...

stories.title: Nasa launches 4 astronauts on world’s first crewed lunar mission in half a century
stories.description: Four astronauts blasted off from Florida on Wednesday on Nasa’s Artemis II mission, a high-stakes voyage around the moon that marks the United States’ boldest step yet toward returning humans to the lunar surface later this decade in a race with China. Nasa’s Space Launch System ...

stories.title: ‘Vile, horrifying, evil’: Democrats demand ceasefire as Trump defends costly war on Iran
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Democrats sharply criticised United States President Donald Trump following his national address on the ongoing war in Iran, framing the conflict as deadly, costly, and lacking clear objectives. In a 19-minute prime-time address from the White House, Trump described t...

stories.title: Indonesia calls for probe into UN peacekeepers’ killing
stories.description: YOGYAKARTA/BEIRUT: Indo­n­esia has called on the United Nations to investigate the deaths of three of its peacekeepers following Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon, a foreign ministry official said on Wednesday as relatives at home mourned their deaths. The ministry’s UN represe...

stories.title: Iran threatens to throw US military back to period before Stone Age — TV
stories.description: According to the report, the Iranian troops "after many years of intensive training are fully ready" to defend their country

stories.title: Iran accuses Israel of attacking desalination plants in Kuwait
stories.description: The statement strongly condemned what it described as an inhumane act

stories.title: Iran’s will power stronger than United States’, says Japanese expert
stories.description: Iran is demonstrating political will and it has no reason to agree to make peace with the United States through a compromise, Ryoji Tateyama pointed out

stories.title: Ukraine continues to receive weapons from US despite Iran operation — Sikorski
stories.description: The Polish foreign minister noted that the situation could change

stories.title: Iran's internet shutdown continues for more than 800 hours — NetBlocks
stories.description: The general public remain cut off from the world without vital updates and without a voice

stories.title: Iran guarantees safety of Indian vessels in Strait of Hormuz — embassy
stories.description: According to media reports, Iran earlier sent a proposal to Oman on joint monitoring of the situation in the Strait of Hormuz in order to ensure safe navigation

stories.title: Japan should lift sanctions before sending delegation to Russia — lawmaker
stories.description: "There is no specific plan to send an economic delegation," Muneo Suzuki said

stories.title: Press review: Trump puts NATO at risk as Houthis threaten to block Bab el-Mandeb
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, April 3rd

stories.title: Iran warns of heavier strikes on US targets if attacks on civilian sites continue
stories.description: On Thursday, Donald Trump demanded that Iran’s authorities accept a deal in the near future and threatened to destroy civilian infrastructure in the Islamic Republic

stories.title: Israel claims to have eliminated 1,000 Hezbollah members in Lebanon over past month
stories.description: The Israeli army has struck more than 3,500 targets

stories.title: Doomsday Clock chief slams US-Israeli operation against Iran as 'absolutely idiotic'
stories.description: John Mecklin believes that the US and Israel are unlikely to achieve the goal of destroying Iran’s nuclear capabilities

stories.title: Doomsday Clock chief points to risk of nuclear weapons use in Iran conflict
stories.description: According to John Mecklin, editor-in-chief of the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, there is currently a "low probability" that the US or Israel could use nuclear weapons on Iran

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
